Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\\n//!\\n//! This module implements:\\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\\n\\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\\nuse std::sync::Arc;\\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\\n\\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\\n    pub body_hash: String,\\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\\n    pub mtask_id: String,\\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\\n    pub expires_at: u64,\\n}\\n\\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\\n    max_entries: usize,\\n    /// TTL in seconds.\\n    ttl_secs: u64,\\n}\\n\\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\\n            max_entries,\\n            ttl_secs,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\\n\\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\\n            } else {\\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\\n            }\\n        }\\n        Ok(None)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\\n\\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\\n                cache.remove(&k);\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        let now = Instant::now();\\n        let expires_at = now\\n            .checked_add(Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| now + Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\\n            .unwrap_or_default()\\n            .as_secs();\\n\\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\\n            body_hash,\\n            mtask_id,\\n            expires_at,\\n        });\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\\n        let now = Instant::now()\\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\\n            .unwrap_or_default()\\n            .as_secs();\\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get current cache size.\\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\\n    /// Index UID.\\n    pub index: String,\\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\\n    pub query_json: String,\\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\\n    pub settings_version: u64,\\n}\\n\\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\\npub struct PendingQuery {\\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\\n    /// When this query was initiated.\\n    started_at: Instant,\\n}\\n\\n/// Query coalescing cache.\\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\\n    window_ms: u64,\\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\\n    max_pending: usize,\\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\\n    max_subscribers: usize,\\n}\\n\\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\\n            window_ms,\\n            max_pending,\\n            max_subscribers,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\\n        &self,\\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\\n\\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\\n            }\\n        }\\n        None\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Register a new pending query.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\\n    pub async fn register(\\n        &self,\\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\\n\\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\\n        }\\n\\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\\n            tx: tx.clone(),\\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\\n        });\\n        Ok(tx)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\\n        let now = Instant::now();\\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get current pending count.\\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n#[cfg(test)]\\nmod tests {\\n    use super::*;\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\\n\\n        // First check should miss.\\n        let result = cache.check(\\\"key1\\\", \\\"hash1\\\").await.unwrap();\\n        assert!(result.is_none());\\n\\n        // Insert.\\n        cache.insert(\\\"key1\\\".into(), \\\"hash1\\\".into(), \\\"mtask-1\\\".into()).await;\\n\\n        // Second check should hit.\\n        let result = cache.check(\\\"key1\\\", \\\"hash1\\\").await.unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\\\"mtask-1\\\".into()));\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\\n\\n        cache.insert(\\\"key1\\\".into(), \\\"hash1\\\".into(), \\\"mtask-1\\\".into()).await;\\n\\n        // Different body hash should error.\\n        let result = cache.check(\\\"key1\\\", \\\"hash2\\\").await;\\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\\n\\n        cache.insert(\\\"key1\\\".into(), \\\"hash1\\\".into(), \\\"mtask-1\\\".into()).await;\\n        cache.insert(\\\"key2\\\".into(), \\\"hash2\\\".into(), \\\"mtask-2\\\".into()).await;\\n        cache.insert(\\\"key3\\\".into(), \\\"hash3\\\".into(), \\\"mtask-3\\\".into()).await;\\n\\n        // At max.\\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\\n\\n        // Adding a 4th should evict the first.\\n        cache.insert(\\\"key4\\\".into(), \\\"hash4\\\".into(), \\\"mtask-4\\\".into()).await;\\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\\n        assert!(cache.check(\\\"key1\\\", \\\"hash1\\\").await.unwrap().is_none());\\n    }\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\\n        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\\n            index: \\\"products\\\".into(),\\n            query_json: r#\\\"{\\\"q\\\":\\\"laptop\\\"}\\\"#.into(),\\n            settings_version: 42,\\n        };\\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\\n            index: \\\"products\\\".into(),\\n            query_json: r#\\\"{\\\"q\\\":\\\"laptop\\\"}\\\"#.into(),\\n            settings_version: 42,\\n        };\\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\\n            index: \\\"products\\\".into(),\\n            query_json: r#\\\"{\\\"q\\\":\\\"phone\\\"}\\\"#.into(),\\n            settings_version: 42,\\n        };\\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\\n            index: \\\"products\\\".into(),\\n            query_json: r#\\\"{\\\"q\\\":\\\"test\\\"}\\\"#.into(),\\n            settings_version: 1,\\n        };\\n\\n        // Should not find a pending query.\\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\\n\\n        // Register and get sender.\\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\\n\\n        // Clean up.\\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_coalescer_hit() {\\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\\n            index: \\\"products\\\".into(),\\n coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict the first.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        assert!(cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn 42,\n        };\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"phone\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n find a pending query.\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\n\n        // Register and get sender.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_hit() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n        tx.send(response.clone()).ok();\n\n        // Receiver should get it.\n        if let Some(mut rx) = rx {\n            let received = rx.recv().await.unwrap();\n            assert_eq!(received, response);\n        }\n Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n//!\n//! This module implements:\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\n\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n            } else {\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n                cache.remove(&k);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        let expires_at = now\n            .checked_add(Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| now + Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n            body_hash,\n            mtask_id,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n        let now = Instant::now()\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cache size.\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n    pub query_json: String,\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict the first.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        assert!(cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn     assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n now find the pending query.\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_some());\n\n        // Broadcast response.\n        let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n {"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"file_path\":\"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/settings.rs\",\"content\":\"//! Two-phase settings broadcast with verification (plan §13.5).\\n//!\\n//! This module implements the propose/verify/commit flow for settings changes,\\n//! replacing the sequential apply-with-rollback approach.\\n\\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\\nuse std::sync::Arc;\\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\\n\\n/// Current phase of a settings broadcast.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n#[repr(u8)]\\npub enum BroadcastPhase {\\n    Idle = 0,\\n    Propose = 1,\\n    Verify = 2,\\n    Commit = 3,\\n}\\n\\n/// Status of an ongoing settings broadcast.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct BroadcastStatus {\\n    /// Index UID.\\n    pub index: String,\\n    /// Current phase.\\n    pub phase: BroadcastPhase,\\n    /// Proposed settings fingerprint.\\n    pub proposed_fingerprint: Option<String>,\\n    /// Per-node task UIDs from Phase 1.\\n    pub node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\\n    /// Per-node verification results from Phase 2.\\n    pub node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\\n    /// Whether verification succeeded.\\n    pub verify_ok: bool,\\n    /// Settings version after commit.\\n    pub settings_version: Option<u64>,\\n    /// Error message if any.\\n    pub error: Option<String>,\\n}\\n\\n/// Settings broadcast coordinator.\\npub struct SettingsBroadcast {\\n    /// In-flight broadcasts (index -> status).\\n    in_flight: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, BroadcastStatus>>>,\\n    /// Global settings version (incremented on successful commit).\\n    settings_version: Arc<RwLock<u64>>,\\n    /// Per-(index, node) settings version (for X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version).\\n    node_settings_version: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<(String, String), u64>>>,\\n}\\n\\nimpl SettingsBroadcast {\\n    /// Create a new settings broadcast coordinator.\\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            in_flight: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\\n            settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),\\n            node_settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get the current global settings version.\\n    pub async fn current_version(&self) -> u64 {\\n        *self.settings_version.read().await\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get the per-(index, node) settings version.\\n    pub async fn node_version(&self, index: &str, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\\n        let versions = self.node_settings_version.read().await;\\n        *versions.get(&(index.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).unwrap_or(&0)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Start a new settings broadcast (Phase 1: Propose).\\n    ///\\n    /// The caller should:\\n    /// 1. PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings on each node in parallel\\n    /// 2. Collect task_uid from each response\\n    /// 3. Call `enter_verify` with the task UIDs\\n    pub async fn start_propose(&self, index: String, settings: &Value) -> Result<String> {\\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\\n\\n        if in_flight.contains_key(&index) {\\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\\n                \\\"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\\\",\\n                index\\n            )));\\n        }\\n\\n        let fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(settings);\\n        let status = BroadcastStatus {\\n            index: index.clone(),\\n            phase: BroadcastPhase::Propose,\\n            proposed_fingerprint: Some(fingerprint),\\n            node_task_uids: HashMap::new(),\\n            node_hashes: HashMap::new(),\\n            verify_ok: false,\\n            settings_version: None,\\n            error: None,\\n        };\\n\\n        in_flight.insert(index.clone(), status);\\n        Ok(index)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Enter Phase 2: Verify.\\n    ///\\n    /// The caller should:\\n    /// 1. Wait for all node tasks to succeed\\n    /// 2. GET /indexes/{uid}/settings from each node\\n    /// 3. Compute SHA256 of canonical JSON for each\\n    /// 4. Call `verify_hashes` with the results\\n    pub async fn enter_verify(\\n        &self,\\n        index: &str,\\n        node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\\n    ) -> Result<()> {\\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\\\"index '{}'\\\", index)))?;\\n\\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Propose {\\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\\\"expected Propose phase\\\".into()));\\n        }\\n\\n        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Verify;\\n        status.node_task_uids = node_task_uids;\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify per-node settings hashes.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if all hashes match the proposed fingerprint.\\n    /// Returns `Err` if any mismatch (caller should retry or abort).\\n    pub async fn verify_hashes(\\n        &self,\\n        index: &str,\\n        node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\\n        expected_fingerprint: &str,\\n    ) -> Result<()> {\\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\\\"index '{}'\\\", index)))?;\\n\\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\\\"expected Verify phase\\\".into()));\\n        }\\n\\n        status.node_hashes = node_hashes.clone();\\n\\n        // Check all hashes match the expected fingerprint.\\n        for (node, hash) in &node_hashes {\\n            if hash != expected_fingerprint {\\n                status.error = Some(format!(\\n                    \\\"node '{}' hash mismatch: expected {}, got {}\\\",\\n                    node, expected_fingerprint, hash\\n                ));\\n                status.verify_ok = false;\\n                return Err(MiroirError::SettingsDivergence);\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        status.verify_ok = true;\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Enter Phase 3: Commit.\\n    ///\\n    /// Increments the global settings version and stamps all affected nodes.\\n    pub async fn commit(&self, index: &str) -> Result<u64> {\\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\\\"index '{}'\\\", index)))?;\\n\\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\\\"expected Verify phase\\\".into()));\\n        }\\n\\n        if !status.verify_ok {\\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\\\"verification failed\\\".into()));\\n        }\\n\\n        // Increment global settings version.\\n        let mut version = self.settings_version.write().await;\\n        *version += 1;\\n        let new_version = *version;\\n        drop(version);\\n\\n        // Update per-node versions for all nodes that verified successfully.\\n        let mut node_versions = self.node_settings_version.write().await;\\n        for node_id in status.node_hashes.keys() {\\n            node_versions.insert((index.to_string(), node_id.clone()), new_version);\\n        }\\n\\n        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Commit;\\n        status.settings_version = Some(new_version);\\n\\n        // Remove from in-flight map after a short delay (caller should do this).\\n        Ok(new_version)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Complete the broadcast and remove from in-flight tracking.\\n    pub async fn complete(&self, index: &str) -> Result<()> {\\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\\n        in_flight.remove(index)\\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\\\"index '{}'\\\", index)))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Abort a broadcast (on error).\\n    pub async fn abort(&self, index: &str, error: String) -> Result<()> {\\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\\n        if let Some(status) = in_flight.get_mut(index) {\\n            status.error = Some(error);\\n        }\\n        in_flight.remove(index)\\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\\\"index '{}'\\\", index)))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get the status of an in-flight broadcast.\\n    pub async fn get_status(&self, index: &str) -> Option<BroadcastStatus> {\\n        self.in_flight.read().await.get(index).cloned()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Check if a broadcast is in-flight for an index.\\n    pub async fn is_in_flight(&self, index: &str) -> bool {\\n        self.in_flight.read().await.contains_key(index)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nimpl Default for SettingsBroadcast {\\n    fn default() -> Self {\\n        Self::new()\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Compute a fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\\nfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\\n    // Canonicalize: sort keys, no extra whitespace.\\n    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\\n        let mut map = serde_json::Map::new();\\n        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\\n            for (key, value) in obj {\\n                map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\\n            }\\n        }\\n        // Sort by key for deterministic output.\\n        let sorted: Vec<_> = map.into_iter().collect();\\n        serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_default()\\n    } else {\\n        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\\n    };\\n\\n    // SHA256 hash.\\n    use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\\n    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\\n    format!(\\\"{:x}\\\", hasher.finalize())\\n}\\n\\n#[cfg(test)]\\nmod tests {\\n    use super::*;\\n    use serde_json::json;\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_fingerprint_settings() {\\n        let settings1 = json!({\\\"rankingRules\\\": [\\\"words\\\", \\\"typo\\\"], \\\"stopWords\\\": [\\\"the\\\", \\\"a\\\"]});\\n        let settings2 = json!({\\\"stopWords\\\": [\\\"a\\\", \\\"the\\\"], \\\"rankingRules\\\": [\\\"typo\\\", \\\"words\\\"]});\\n\\n        // Order-independent canonicalization should produce same fingerprint.\\n        let fp1 = fingerprint_settings(&settings1);\\n        let fp2 = fingerprint_settings(&settings2);\\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_broadcast_full_flow() {\\n        let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::new();\\n\\n        // Start propose.\\n        let index = \\\"products\\\".to_string();\\n        let settings = json!({\\\"rankingRules\\\": [\\\"words\\\"]});\\n        let fp = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\\n        broadcast.start_propose(index.clone(), &settings).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        // Enter verify with node task UIDs.\\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\\n        node_tasks.insert(\\\"node-1\\\".to_string(), 100);\\n        node_tasks.insert(\\\"node-2\\\".to_string(), 101);\\n        broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        // Verify hashes.\\n        let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\\n        node_hashes.insert(\\\"node-1\\\".to_string(), fp.clone());\\n        node_hashes.insert(\\\"node-2\\\".to_string(), fp.clone());\\n        broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &fp).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        // Commit.\\n        let new_version = broadcast.commit(&index).await.unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(new_version, 1);\\n\\n        // Complete.\\n        broadcast.complete(&index).await.unwrap();\\n        assert!(!broadcast.is_in_flight(&index).await);\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_broadcast_hash_mismatch() {\\n        let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::new();\\n\\n        let index = \\\"products\\\".to_string();\\n        let settings = json!({\\\"rankingRules\\\": [\\\"words\\\"]});\\n        let fp = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\\n        broadcast.start_propose(index.clone(), &settings).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\\n        node_tasks.insert(\\\"node-1\\\".to_string(), 100);\\n        broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        // Wrong hash.\\n        let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\\n        node_hashes.insert(\\\"node-1\\\".to_string(), \\\"wrong_hash\\\".to_string());\\n        let result = broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &fp).await;\\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::SettingsDivergence)));\\n\\n        // Status should reflect the error.\\n        let status = broadcast.get_status(&index).await;\\n        assert!(status.unwrap().error.is_some());\\n    }\\n\\n    #[tokio::test]\\n    async fn test_node_version_tracking() {\\n        let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::new();\\n\\n        // Initially zero.\\n        assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(\\\"products\\\", \\\"node-1\\\").await, 0);\\n\\n        // After commit, version should be tracked.\\n        let index = \\\"products\\\".to_string();\\n        let settings = json!({\\\"rankingRules\\\": [\\\"words\\\"]});\\n        let fp = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\\n        broadcast.start_propose(index.clone(), &settings).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\\n        node_tasks.insert(\\\"node-1\\\".to_string(), 100);\\n        broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\\n        node_hashes.insert(\\\"node-1\\\".to_string(), fp.clone());\\n        broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &fp).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        broadcast.commit(&index).await.unwrap();\\n\\n        // Node version should now be 1.\\n        assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(\\\"products\\\", \\\"node-1\\\").await, 1);\\n    }\\n}\\n\"}"}},"session_id":"ebcd54be-87a0-4c79-9623-38f1d6d4503f","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"f0b6adb8-c037-412d-8132-3091fcd21000"}

   /// The caller should:\n    /// 1. PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings on each node in parallel\n    /// 2. Collect task_uid from each response\n    /// 3. Call `enter_verify` with the task UIDs\n    pub async fn start_propose(&self, index: String, settings: &Value) -> Result<String> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n\n        if in_flight.contains_key(&index) {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\n                \"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\",\n                index\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(settings);\n        let status = BroadcastStatus {\n            index: index.clone(),\n            phase: BroadcastPhase::Propose,\n            proposed_fingerprint: Some(fingerprint),\n            node_task_uids: HashMap::new(),\n            node_hashes: HashMap::new(),\n            verify_ok: false,\n            settings_version: None,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        in_flight.insert(index.clone(), status);\n        Ok(index)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter Phase 2: Verify.\n    ///\n    /// The caller should:\n    /// 1. Wait for all node tasks to succeed\n    /// 2. GET /indexes/{uid}/settings from each node\n    /// 3. Compute SHA256 of canonical JSON for each\n    /// 4. Call `verify_hashes` with the results\n    pub async fn enter_verify(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Propose {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Propose phase\".into()));\n        }\n\n        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Verify;\n        status.node_task_uids = node_task_uids;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Verify per-node settings hashes.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if all hashes match the proposed fingerprint.\n    /// Returns `Err` if any mismatch (caller should retry or abort).\n    pub async fn verify_hashes(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\n        expected_fingerprint: &str,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Verify phase\".into()));\n        }\n\n        status.node_hashes = node_hashes.clone();\n\n        // Check all hashes match the expected fingerprint.\n        for (node, hash) in &node_hashes {\n            if hash != expected_fingerprint {\n                status.error = Some(format!(\n                    \"node '{}' hash mismatch: expected {}, got {}\",\n                    node, expected_fingerprint, hash\n                ));\n                status.verify_ok = false;\n                return Err(MiroirError::SettingsDivergence);\n            }\n        }\n\n        status.verify_ok = true;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Enter Phase 3: Commit.\n    ///\n    /// Increments the global settings version and stamps all affected nodes.\n    pub async fn commit(&self, index: &str) -> Result<u64> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Verify phase\".into()));\n        }\n\n        if !status.verify_ok {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"verification failed\".into()));\n        }\n\n        // Increment global settings version.\n        let mut version = self.settings_version.write().await;\n        *version += 1;\n        let new_version = *version;\n        drop(version);\n\n        // Update per-node versions for all nodes that verified successfully.\n        let mut node_versions = self.node_settings_version.write().await;\n        for node_id in status.node_hashes.keys() {\n            node_versions.insert((index.to_string(), node_id.clone()), new_version);\n        }\n\n        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Commit;\n        status.settings_version = Some(new_version);\n\n        // Remove from in-flight map after a short delay (caller should do this).\n        Ok(new_version)\n    }\n\n    /// Complete the broadcast and remove from in-flight tracking.\n    pub async fn complete(&self, index: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        in_flight.remove(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Abort a broadcast (on error).\n    pub async fn abort(&self, index: &str, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        if let Some(status) = in_flight.get_mut(index) {\n            status.error = Some(error);\n        }\n        in_flight.remove(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get the status of an in-flight broadcast.\n    pub async fn get_status(&self, index: &str) -> Option<BroadcastStatus> {\n        self.in_flight.read().await.get(index).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a broadcast is in-flight for an index.\n    pub async fn is_in_flight(&self, index: &str) -> bool {\n        self.in_flight.read().await.contains_key(index)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SettingsBroadcast {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute a fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\nfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\n    // Canonicalize: sort keys, no extra whitespace.\n    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\n        let mut map = serde_json::Map::new();\n        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\n            for (key, value) in obj {\n                map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        // Sort by key for deterministic output.\n        let sorted: Vec<_> = map.into_iter().collect();\n        serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_default()\n    } else {\n        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n    };\n\n    // SHA256 hash.\n    use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_fingerprint_settings() {\n        let settings1 = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\"], \"stopWords\": [\"the\", \"a\"]});\n        let settings2 = json!({\"stopWords\": [\"a\", \"the\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"typo\", \"words\"]});\n\n        // Order-independent canonicalization should produce same fingerprint.\n        let fp1 = fingerprint_settings(&settings1);\n        let fp2 = fingerprint_settings(&settings2);\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_broadcast_full_flow() {\n        let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::new();\n\n        // Start propose.\n        let index = This module implements the propose/verify/commit flow for settings changes,\n//! replacing the sequential apply-with-rollback approach.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Current phase of a settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum BroadcastPhase {\n    Idle = 0,\n    Propose = 1,\n    Verify = 2,\n    Commit = 3,\n}\n\n/// Status of an ongoing settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BroadcastStatus {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: BroadcastPhase,\n    /// settings_version: Option<u64>,\n    /// Error message if any.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Settings broadcast coordinator.\npub struct SettingsBroadcast {\n    /// In-flight broadcasts settings broadcast coordinator.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            in_flight: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),\n            node_settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current global settings version.\n    pub async fn current_version(&self) -> u64 {\n self.node_settings_version.read().await;\n        *versions.get(&(index.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).unwrap_or(&0)\n    }\n\n    /// Start a new settings broadcast (Phase 1: Propose).\n    ///\n Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n//!\n//! This module implements:\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\n\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n            } else {\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n                cache.remove(&k);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        let expires_at = now\n            .checked_add(Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| now + Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n            body_hash,\n            mtask_id,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n        let now = Instant::now()\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cache size.\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n    pub query_json: String,\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict the first.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        assert!(cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\n        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"phone\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Should not find a pending query.\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\n\n        // Register and get sender.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_hit() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Register.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should now find the pending query.\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_some());\n\n        // Broadcast response.\n        let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n        tx.send(response.clone()).ok();\n\n        // Receiver should get it.\n        if let Some(mut rx) = rx {\n            let received = rx.recv().await.unwrap();\n            assert_eq!(received, response);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":326,"startLine":1,"totalLines":326}}}

Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n//!\n//! This module implements:\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\n\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n            } else {\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n                cache.remove(&k);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        let expires_at = now\n            .checked_add(Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| now + Duration::from_secs(self.ttl_secs))\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n            body_hash,\n            mtask_id,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n        let now = Instant::now()\n            .duration_since(Instant::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cache size.\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n    pub query_json: String,\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict the first.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        assert!(cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\n        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"phone\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Should not find a pending query.\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\n\n        // Register and get sender.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_hit() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Register.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should now find the pending query.\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_some());\n\n        // Broadcast response.\n        let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n        tx.send(response.clone()).ok();\n\n        // Receiver should get it.\n        if let Some(mut rx) = rx {\n            let received = rx.recv().await.unwrap();\n            assert_eq!(received, response);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":8,"oldLines":7,"newStart":8,"newLines":7,"lines":[" use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};"," use std::collections::HashMap;"," use
